steps.title: Identify the angle pair from the figure
steps.content: When transversal $n$ crosses parallel lines $r$ and $s$, the eight angles formed split into two groups of congruent angles. From the figure, the $162°$ angle at line $r$ and the $x°$ angle at line $s$ are positioned so they are congruent (a corresponding, alternate-interior, alternate-exterior, or vertical-angle pair) rather than supplementary.

steps.title: Apply the congruent-angle relationship
steps.content: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, congruent angle pairs (corresponding, alternate interior, alternate exterior, vertical) have equal measures. Therefore, the $162°$ angle at line $r$ equals the $x°$ angle at line $s$:$$x = 162$$
